title: CLI trusts foreign-owned predictable /tmp socket dirs

findingDetails.evidence: `ensureSocketEnvironment()` defaults/uses the socket dir, creates it, then only rejects `(stat.mode & 0o022) !== 0` at lines 753-763 before `callTool()` probes/connects to `bridge.getIpcSocketPath(...)` at lines 924-935. The same ownerless check exists in `system-code-graph/mcp_server/code-index-cli.ts:924-934` and `system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/skill-advisor-cli.ts:1017-1027`. The warm fallback helpers only check socket existence/length before `probeDaemon(..., deepProbe:true)` (`hooks/spec-memory-cli-fallback.ts:83-111`, `hooks/code-index-cli-fallback.ts:82-110`, `skill-advisor-cli-fallback.ts:218-238`).
findingDetails.why: The default socket dirs are predictable under `/tmp`. A local attacker can pre-create `/tmp/mk-<service>` owned by themselves with a non-writable but traversable mode such as 0755 and expose a permissive fake `daemon-ipc.sock`. Because the client path does not require the directory to be owned by the current uid before probing, warm-only hooks and CLIs can classify the fake endpoint as warm and send memory/advisor/code request payloads to it.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a shared client-side socket-path validation helper before every probe/connect/spawn: canonicalize the parent directory, require `stat.uid === process.getuid()` when available, reject group/world-writable dirs, and reject symlinked socket paths. Reuse it from all three CLIs and hook fallback helpers, and add tests for foreign-owned 0755 `/tmp/mk-*` dirs.
